title: Deterministic repo export of Role Mailbox to .GOV/roles_shared/exports/role_mailbox/ (always-on)
goal: Implement the always-on deterministic mailbox-to-repo exporter that mirrors authoritative Postgres state into .GOV/roles_shared/exports/role_mailbox/ as JSON Lines append-only thread files plus a thread index, preserving the folded WP-1-Role-Mailbox-v1 stub intent (LLM role-to-role coordination with auditable governance, chat-is-not-state).
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox/exporter.rs with `MailboxExporter::export(repo, target_dir)` writing thread_index.json + per-thread JSONL files; integration tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/role_mailbox_exporter_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-177 repo provides the read surface the exporter consumes.
implementation_notes: Per spec v02.186 §05-security-and-observability.md lines 5904 and 5962-5967 (Role Mailbox structured projections [ADD v02.168]) and the folded stub intent (always-on deterministic repo export to .GOV/ROLE_MAILBOX/): exporter writes (a) `thread_index.json` containing the compact summary contract (thread_id, title, lifecycle_state, linked_record_kind, linked_record_id, updated_at_utc, message_count) for every non-archived thread, sorted by updated_at_utc descending; (b) per-thread file `threads/<thread_id>.jsonl` with one JSON object per message line in chronological order. Append-only semantics: if a thread file exists, only NEW messages (since the last exported message_id watermark stored in `<thread_id>.watermark`) are appended; existing lines never rewritten. Idempotency: running exporter twice with no new messages writes nothing. Deterministic: same inputs produce byte-identical outputs (stable key ordering via BTreeMap, stable line ordering by message created_at_utc then message_id). The target path is configurable via `MailboxExporterConfig { target_dir: PathBuf }` defaulting to `.GOV/roles_shared/exports/role_mailbox/` relative to the repo root discovered via the same project-root helper used by KERNEL-002. Honors GLOBAL-PORTABILITY-004 (no hardcoded absolute paths). Tests: (a) export an empty repo, expect thread_index.json with []; (b) create 3 threads with 2 messages each, export, expect 3 files + 6 lines; (c) re-run with no new state, expect no file writes (mtime unchanged); (d) add 1 new message to thread 2, re-run, expect only thread-2.jsonl appended by 1 line; (e) goldenfile cross-check on a fixture set.
risk_if_missed: Without deterministic export, the operator loses the auditable repo-side mailbox view; the folded WP-1-Role-Mailbox-v1 stub's 'always-on deterministic repo export to .GOV/ROLE_MAILBOX/' intent is dropped; LLM-to-LLM coordination loses its safety net.
validator_focus: Exporter is idempotent (no-op when state unchanged), append-only (never rewrites existing lines), deterministic (golden-fixture byte-identical), and uses repo-root discovery not hardcoded paths.

implementation_or_hardening_notes: Normalized handoff expires_at_utc to Postgres microsecond precision before bundle hashing.

note: MT-177 targeted repository lifecycle round trip passed.

postgres_test_url_live_evidence_20260525.summary: Role Mailbox V1 live Postgres suites passed, including repository lifecycle, lease, backpressure, handoff, and exporter coverage.
residual_risks: Supporting evidence only; Integration Validator verdict remains PENDING.
Focused Postgres/live-kernel commands were run, not a full workspace cargo test.

minimum_controls: Goldenfile cross-check test asserts byte-identical output for a fixed seed.
Append-only invariant verified by mtime + line-count assertions across multiple re-runs.
Exporter resolves target_dir via repo-root helper (no `D:\` or `/home/` literals; GLOBAL-PORTABILITY-005).

implementation_supporting_evidence_20260.summary: MT-178 Role Mailbox deterministic always-on exporter transitioned PENDING -> CLAIMED -> READY_FOR_VALIDATION. The existing src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox_v1/exporter.rs (MailboxExporter::new(MailboxExporterConfig), MailboxExporterConfig::default_under_repo_root, deterministic sort by updated_at_utc DESC with BTreeMap key-stability, per-thread JSONL appends with watermark cursor, idempotency via path_content_equals on thread_index, archived-thread filtering, ExporterError typed via thiserror) was reviewed and accepted; no in-place edits to the exporter module. A dedicated MT-178 adversarial test file was added at tests/role_mailbox_exporter_tests.rs (the contract-named path) covering: (a) default path matches the MT-178 + CX-218 .GOV/roles_shared/exports/role_mailbox/ contract path under repo root, (b) GLOBAL-PORTABILITY-005 negative-surface check that no `D:\` or `/home/` or `/Users/` literal appears in the default config, (c) always-on semantics — empty repo still writes `thread_index.json: []`, (d) goldenfile / byte-identical-across-runs deterministic export with fixed timestamps, (e) thread_index.json sorted by updated_at_utc DESC, (f) filename convention `threads/<uuid>.jsonl` + `threads/<uuid>.watermark`, (g) chronological line ordering inside each thread JSONL with message_id tie-break, (h) append-only invariant via byte-prefix snapshot before+after a re-export, (i) idempotency over identical input, (j) archived threads filtered from index, (k) index `message_count` equals per-thread JSONL line count (no silent drops), (l) cross-run index stability when only message_count changes, (m) ExporterError::Io surfaced on uncreatable target_dir instead of panic, (n) constructor surface lock (no opt-out/dry-run flag), plus Postgres-gated adversarial tests: three-thread two-message round-trip with byte-identical output across two fresh target dirs, no-silent-drops cross-check between repo list_thread_messages and JSONL line count, interleaved-thread deterministic (created_at_utc, message_id) ordering, incremental-append-only of exactly N new lines to the affected thread with unaffected file byte-identical, archived-thread excluded from index while JSONL preserved for audit.

out_of_scope_edits.change: Added [[test]] block declaring role_mailbox_exporter_tests with required-features=['test-utils'] mirroring the role_mailbox_repo_tests / role_mailbox_lease_tests pattern. Necessary because the test binary references RoleMailboxRepository which is gated behind the test-utils feature; without an explicit [[test]] entry Cargo auto-discovery would not gate the feature correctly. Same pattern accepted in MT-177 implementation_supporting_evidence_20260523_mt177r2.out_of_scope_edits.

implementation_supporting_evidence_20260.contract_deviation_note: MT-178 expected_diff_shape and owned_files name `src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox/exporter.rs`, but the existing folded implementation lives under role_mailbox_v1/exporter.rs (the same PATH_SHAPE_DRIFT class flagged in MT-176 implementation_supporting_evidence_20260521.contract_deviation_note and MT-177 implementation_supporting_evidence_20260523_mt177r2.contract_deviation_note). No new product code under role_mailbox/exporter.rs was created — that would duplicate the authoritative implementation. Operator/Integration Validator decision needed on path-shape drift vs. the actual role_mailbox_v1/ tree.

proof_commands.note: 14/14 default tests pass; 5 Postgres-gated tests #[ignore]-ed pending POSTGRES_TEST_URL.

proof_commands.command: cargo test --features test-utils --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --test role_mailbox_v1_tests --test role_mailbox_repo_tests --test role_mailbox_exporter_tests -- --nocapture --test-threads=1
proof_commands.note: Adjacent role_mailbox_v1_tests (9 passed/2 ignored) + role_mailbox_repo_tests (7 passed/11 ignored) + role_mailbox_exporter_tests (14 passed/5 ignored) all pass alongside the new MT-178 test binary; no regression from the Cargo.toml [[test]] addition.

residual_risks: 5 Postgres-gated adversarial tests (byte-identical round-trip, no-silent-drops index/JSONL cross-check, (created_at, message_id) ordering, incremental-append-only, archived-thread JSONL preservation) require POSTGRES_TEST_URL to actually validate the in-database semantics. Without the live-Postgres run the contract's red_team.minimum_controls #1 + #2 are structurally satisfied by the pure-Rust deterministic tests but not executionally verified against the real repo path — same Postgres-gating class as MT-177 / MT-190 / MT-197.
PATH_SHAPE_DRIFT: owned_files name role_mailbox/exporter.rs but the implementation is under role_mailbox_v1/exporter.rs (folded preservation slice). Integration Validator decision needed.

PATH_HELPER_DRIFT: `RuntimeGovernancePaths::role_mailbox_export_dir` resolves to `.handshake/gov/ROLE_MAILBOX/` (runtime governance root), but the MT-178 / CX-218 contract anchors the exporter at `.GOV/roles_shared/exports/role_mailbox/` (repo source root). These are two different directories. The exporter is contract-correct here; the helper is used by `crate::api::role_mailbox` for a different (read-only) read surface. Out of MT-178 scope to reconcile; flagged for downstream cleanup.

Cargo.toml [[test]] addition is out-of-owned-files; operator waiver requested. The alternative — Cargo auto-discovery — would silently default to feature set [] and bypass the test-utils requirement that other role_mailbox_v1 tests depend on.

Default test run executes 14 pure-Rust assertions in <2s; Postgres-gated tests require an additional `cargo test -- --ignored` run with POSTGRES_TEST_URL set. WP Integration Validator should plan for that run as part of the WP-KERNEL-004 closeout gate.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: Always-on deterministic exporter writes BTreeMap-sorted thread_index.json + per-thread JSONL with watermark-driven incremental append-only semantics; goldenfile + 14 always-on adversarial assertions cover the append-only and determinism contracts; no hardcoded paths.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: Always-on deterministic exporter writes BTreeMap-sorted thread_index.json + per-thread JSONL with watermark-driven incremental append-only semantics; goldenfile + 14 always-on adversarial assertions cover the append-only and determinism contracts; no hardcoded paths.
